data_coverage_summary: Dozuki API v2.0 exposes work-instruction, training, approval, user, team, skills, signoff, and work-log resources used to manage digital manufacturing documentation and operator workflows.
buyer_guidance: Use Dozuki when you need API access to digital work instructions, training completion, signoffs, reviews, and execution logs around operator-facing procedures. It supports equipment-adjacent content and work execution, but not machine telemetry.
overview: Dozuki's public API v2.0 docs describe a JSON-first REST surface for guides, wikis, courses, users, teams, approvals, periodic review, quizzes, skills, step forms, webhooks, and work-log execution. The documentation explicitly supports GET, POST, PATCH, PUT, and DELETE across guide, course, team, and work-log resources, including guide-step editing, course assignment, signoff workflows, skills ratings, and execution-state updates for in-progress work. That makes the API useful for structured asset or procedure content and manufacturing workflow writeback, but not for telemetry streams. Asset data is Supported. Telemetry data is Unsupported. Writeback is Supported.

available_data.category: Instructional content, approvals, and content hierarchy
available_data.description: Dozuki exposes the content model and governance surfaces used to author and manage digital procedures and related knowledge objects.
data_points: Tags, releases, and periodic review schedules
Media attachments and translation glossary entries

available_data.category: Training, teams, skills, and execution logs
available_data.description: The API also covers workforce enablement and actual guided-work execution data for assigned users and teams.
data_points: Work-log entries with start, pause, resume, finish, suspend, and step-data updates

ingest_considerations: Dozuki uses revision IDs on versioned content updates, so write flows that edit guides, wikis, or related resources need optimistic-concurrency handling rather than blind overwrites.

The API is strongly workflow-oriented around documentation, training, approvals, and work execution, so downstream systems should model it as an operational knowledge and execution platform rather than a sensor or machine-data source.
No exact downloadable OpenAPI or Swagger artifact was confirmed during this review; the public reference is comprehensive, but machine-readable spec evidence remains outstanding.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: The public Dozuki API v2.0 reference documents guides, courses, approvals, users, teams, skills, step forms, webhooks, periodic review, quizzes, wikis, and work-log execution endpoints with GET, POST, PATCH, PUT, and DELETE operations.
